predictions.reasoning: Flat centrality distribution (degree std=0.49 < 1.5). Cannot meaningfully distinguish hub nodes from periphery. This mirrors the bipartite Reddit graph result where TAR failed because degree distribution was too uniform.
High label-neighbor density (1.00 > 0.4) — nodes sharing hyperedges tend to share labels, so replaying nodes preserves community structure.
Non-trivial hypergraph structure (bipartite score=0.37). Topology-agnostic methods serve as baseline; structure-aware methods may outperform.

Novel contribution: structure-aware Fisher weighting by hyperedge participation. Even with flat degree distribution, the size-normalized weighting (|e|^{-1}) may capture important structural importance signals that standard EWC misses.
